entries.description: Include the copyright notice and permission notice in all copies or substantial portions of the software
entries.compliance_action: Copy the MIT license text (including the copyright line) into your LICENSE file, NOTICE file, or documentation distributed with the software

entries.description: The full license text must accompany the software in all distributions, whether source or binary
entries.compliance_action: Include the complete MIT license text in your distribution. A LICENSE file in the repository root is sufficient for source distributions; for binaries, include it in documentation or a notices directory

entries.description: Retain all copyright, patent, trademark, and attribution notices from the original source
entries.compliance_action: Preserve all existing copyright headers in source files. Include the original NOTICE file contents in your distribution

entries.description: Include a copy of the Apache 2.0 license text with every distribution
entries.compliance_action: Ship the full Apache-2.0 license text in a LICENSE file. If the original work includes a NOTICE file, its contents must also be included

entries.description: Modified files must carry prominent notices stating that you changed them
entries.compliance_action: Add a note at the top of each modified file (e.g., 'Modified by [name] on [date]') or document changes in a CHANGELOG

entries.description: Contributors grant a perpetual, worldwide, royalty-free patent license for their contributions. The grant terminates if you file a patent lawsuit alleging infringement by the work
entries.compliance_action: No action required to receive the grant. Avoid filing patent claims against the software, as this terminates your patent license

entries.description: The license does not grant permission to use the trade names, trademarks, service marks, or product names of the licensor
entries.compliance_action: Do not use the original project's name, logo, or trademarks to endorse or promote your derived product without separate written permission

entries.description: Retain all copyright notices in the source code and any accompanying files
entries.compliance_action: Keep all existing copyright headers intact in source files. Include original copyright information in your distribution

entries.description: When distributing binaries, you must make the complete corresponding source code available under GPL-2.0
entries.compliance_action: Offer the complete source code alongside the binary distribution, or provide a written offer (valid for 3 years) to supply the source on request. The source must include all modifications and build scripts

entries.description: Any work that is a derivative work (linked, combined, or modified) must also be licensed under GPL-2.0. The entire combined program must be released under GPL-2.0
entries.compliance_action: License all derivative and combined works under GPL-2.0-only. This applies to static and dynamic linking in most interpretations. Separate programs communicating via pipes or sockets are generally not considered derivative works

entries.description: Each source file should include a copyright notice and a reference to the GPL-2.0 license. Interactive programs must display a notice at startup
entries.compliance_action: Include a copyright header and GPL-2.0 reference in each source file. For interactive programs, display the warranty disclaimer and license pointer at startup

entries.description: Retain all copyright notices in the source code and any accompanying files
entries.compliance_action: Keep all existing copyright headers intact in source files. Include original copyright information in your distribution

entries.description: When distributing binaries, you must make the complete corresponding source code available under GPL-2.0 or any later version at your choice
entries.compliance_action: Offer the complete source code alongside the binary distribution, or provide a written offer (valid for 3 years) to supply the source. You may choose to distribute under GPL-2.0, GPL-3.0, or any later version published by the FSF

entries.description: Derivative works must be licensed under GPL-2.0 or any later version. The 'or later' clause allows you to upgrade to GPL-3.0+ if desired
entries.compliance_action: License derivative works under GPL-2.0-or-later, or choose a specific later version (e.g., GPL-3.0-only). The version upgrade is at the distributor's discretion

entries.description: Include the 'or later version' language in copyright headers to preserve the version upgrade option for downstream recipients
entries.compliance_action: Use the standard FSF header: 'This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software Foundation; either version 2 of the License, or (at your option) any later version.'

entries.description: Retain all copyright notices, license notices, and warranty disclaimers in the source and object code
entries.compliance_action: Keep all existing copyright headers and legal notices intact. Include original copyright information in all distributions

entries.description: When distributing object code, you must provide the Corresponding Source (all source needed to build, install, and run the program, including build scripts and installation instructions)
entries.compliance_action: Distribute the Corresponding Source alongside the object code, or provide a durable URL where the source can be downloaded. For network distribution, the source must remain available for as long as the binary is offered

entries.description: All derivative works, including works linked against GPL-3.0 libraries, must be licensed under GPL-3.0. The copyleft scope covers the entire combined work
entries.compliance_action: License all derivative and combined works under GPL-3.0-only. If combining with code under a GPL-compatible license, the combined work must still be distributed under GPL-3.0

entries.description: Each contributor grants a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free patent license for their contribution. Distributors may not impose further patent restrictions on recipients
entries.compliance_action: Do not impose additional patent licensing fees on GPL-3.0 software you distribute. If you receive a patent license that is not extendable to all downstream recipients, you may not distribute the covered work

entries.description: Include appropriate copyright notices, a warranty disclaimer, and a pointer to the full GPL-3.0 text. Interactive programs must display this at startup
entries.compliance_action: Add a GPL-3.0 header to each source file. For interactive programs, display the copyright notice, warranty disclaimer, and license reference on startup

entries.description: Modified files must carry prominent notices giving the date of change and indicating that the file was modified
entries.compliance_action: Add a notice to each modified file stating the date and nature of the change. Example: 'Modified by [name] on [date]: [description of changes]'

entries.description: Retain all copyright notices, license notices, and warranty disclaimers
entries.compliance_action: Keep all existing copyright headers and legal notices intact in all distributions

entries.description: Provide the Corresponding Source when distributing object code, under GPL-3.0 or any later version at your choice
entries.compliance_action: Distribute the Corresponding Source alongside the object code or via a durable URL. You may choose to distribute under GPL-3.0 or any later version published by the FSF

entries.description: Derivative works must be licensed under GPL-3.0 or any later version. The 'or later' clause allows upgrading to future GPL versions
entries.compliance_action: License derivative works under GPL-3.0-or-later, or choose a specific later version. The version upgrade decision is at the distributor's discretion

entries.description: Same patent grant and anti-patent-aggression provisions as GPL-3.0-only
entries.compliance_action: Do not impose additional patent licensing fees. The patent grant applies under whichever GPL version you distribute under

entries.description: Include the 'or later version' language in copyright headers to preserve the version upgrade option
entries.compliance_action: Use the standard FSF header: 'This program is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later version.'

entries.description: Modified files must carry prominent notices giving the date of change
entries.compliance_action: Add a notice to each modified file stating the date and nature of the change

entries.description: Retain all copyright notices and license references in the library source code
entries.compliance_action: Keep all existing copyright headers and legal notices intact in the library source files

entries.description: When distributing binaries of the library itself (or modified versions), you must provide the complete source code of the LGPL-licensed library. Applications that merely link to the library are not required to disclose their own source
entries.compliance_action: Distribute the library source alongside binaries, or provide a written offer valid for 3 years. Only the LGPL-licensed library source must be disclosed, not the proprietary application source

entries.description: End users must be able to relink the application with a modified version of the LGPL library. This means either dynamic linking or providing object files sufficient for relinking
entries.compliance_action: Use dynamic linking (shared libraries / .so / .dll) to the LGPL component, OR provide the object files or source of your application sufficient to relink with a modified version of the library

entries.description: Include a copy of both the LGPL-2.1 and GPL-2.0 license texts, as LGPL-2.1 incorporates GPL-2.0 by reference
entries.compliance_action: Ship both the LGPL-2.1 and GPL-2.0 license texts in your distribution. Note that the user has the option to treat the library under GPL-2.0 instead of LGPL-2.1

entries.description: Retain all copyright notices and license references in the library source code
entries.compliance_action: Keep all existing copyright headers and legal notices intact in the library source files

entries.description: When distributing object code of the library, provide the Corresponding Source of the library. Applications that link to the library must also provide the Corresponding Application Code (enough to relink)
entries.compliance_action: Provide the library source code and, for applications, the object code or source sufficient to relink the application with a modified version of the library

entries.description: Users must be able to modify the LGPL library and relink the combined work. The application must support relinking with a user-modified version of the library
entries.compliance_action: Use dynamic linking, or provide object files and build instructions sufficient for the user to relink the application with a modified library. Encryption keys for installation must be provided if applicable

entries.description: Include copies of both the LGPL-3.0 and GPL-3.0 license texts, as LGPL-3.0 is defined as additional permissions on top of GPL-3.0
entries.compliance_action: Ship both the LGPL-3.0 and GPL-3.0 license texts. The user has the option to treat the library under GPL-3.0 instead of LGPL-3.0

entries.description: Inherits the GPL-3.0 patent grant. Contributors grant a royalty-free patent license for their contributions to the library
entries.compliance_action: Do not impose additional patent restrictions on the LGPL library. The same anti-patent-aggression rules from GPL-3.0 apply

entries.description: Redistributions in source or binary form must retain the copyright notice, the list of conditions, and the disclaimer
entries.compliance_action: Include the original copyright notice and the two-clause BSD license text in your LICENSE file or documentation. For binary distributions, include it in the documentation or NOTICE file shipped with the product

entries.description: Redistributions in source or binary form must retain the copyright notice, the list of conditions, and the disclaimer
entries.compliance_action: Include the original copyright notice and the three-clause BSD license text in your LICENSE file or documentation

entries.description: Neither the name of the copyright holder nor the names of its contributors may be used to endorse or promote products derived from this software without prior written permission
entries.compliance_action: Do not use the original author's name or the project name to endorse or promote your derived product unless you have received explicit written permission

entries.description: Retain all copyright, patent, trademark, and attribution notices in the source form of the covered software
entries.compliance_action: Keep all existing copyright headers and attribution notices in MPL-licensed source files

entries.description: If you modify any MPL-licensed file, you must make the source of that specific file available under the MPL. The copyleft scope is per-file, not per-project
entries.compliance_action: Distribute the source of any modified MPL-2.0 files alongside your distribution, or make it available via a URL for at least 12 months. Unmodified MPL files and your own new files are not affected

entries.description: Include a copy of the MPL-2.0 license text with every distribution. Each MPL-licensed file must retain its license header
entries.compliance_action: Ship the MPL-2.0 license text in your distribution. Do not remove the MPL header from any covered file

entries.description: Retain all copyright notices, license notices, and warranty disclaimers
entries.compliance_action: Keep all existing copyright headers and legal notices intact in all distributions

entries.description: When distributing object code, provide the Corresponding Source. Same requirements as GPL-3.0
entries.compliance_action: Distribute the Corresponding Source alongside the object code or via a durable URL

entries.description: All derivative works must be licensed under AGPL-3.0. The copyleft scope is the same as GPL-3.0 for distributed software
entries.compliance_action: License all derivative and combined works under AGPL-3.0-only

entries.description: Include appropriate copyright notices and a license reference. Interactive programs (including network services) must offer access to the source
entries.compliance_action: Add AGPL-3.0 headers to source files. For web interfaces, provide a prominent link (e.g., in the page footer or an 'About' section) where users can download the complete source code

entries.description: If users interact with the software over a network (e.g., a web application or API), you must offer those users the Corresponding Source of the running version. This is the key difference from GPL-3.0
entries.compliance_action: Provide a download link for the complete source code accessible to all network users. The source must correspond to the exact version running on the server. A common approach is an /about or /source endpoint serving a tarball or linking to a tagged repository

entries.description: The copyright notice and the permission notice must appear in all copies of the software or substantial portions thereof
entries.compliance_action: Include the ISC license text (with the copyright line) in your LICENSE file or documentation. The ISC license is functionally equivalent to a simplified MIT/BSD-2-Clause

entries.description: Retain all copyright notices, license notices, and disclaimer of warranties in the source code
entries.compliance_action: Keep all existing copyright headers and the EUPL-1.2 license reference in source files

entries.description: When distributing the work in object code or executable form, provide the source code, or indicate a repository where the source is freely available
entries.compliance_action: Distribute the source alongside object code, or provide a URL to a public repository where the source code is available

entries.description: Derivative works must be licensed under EUPL-1.2 or a compatible license listed in the EUPL-1.2 Appendix (GPL-2.0, GPL-3.0, AGPL-3.0, LGPL-2.1, LGPL-3.0, MPL-2.0, CECILL-2.1, EPL-2.0, and others)
entries.compliance_action: License derivative works under EUPL-1.2 or one of the compatible licenses listed in the EUPL-1.2 Appendix. The compatibility list makes EUPL-1.2 one of the most interoperable copyleft licenses

entries.description: Include a copy of the EUPL-1.2 license text with every distribution. The EUPL is officially available in 23 EU languages, all equally authentic
entries.compliance_action: Ship the EUPL-1.2 license text (in your chosen language version) in your distribution

entries.description: When distributing a modified version, you must indicate clearly that the work has been modified and state the date of modification
entries.compliance_action: Add a notice to modified files or the distribution indicating the modification date and nature of changes

entries.description: The Unlicense is a public domain dedication. There are no obligations: no attribution, no copyleft, no source disclosure. The software is released into the public domain
entries.compliance_action: No compliance action required. You may use, modify, and distribute the software without restriction. Note: public domain status may not be recognized in all jurisdictions; in those cases the Unlicense includes a permissive fallback license
